pass_framing: recreatability-and-integration-gap cleanup pass surfacing 6 items (Q1-Q6) labelled with the NLSpec failure-mode framework. Q1 (major gap — built-in templates lack sufficient specifications for agentic regeneration) was captured in a prior session before the continuation; Q2-Q6 were surfaced during a bootstrap-plan review against PROPOSAL.md v017 and user-driven triage from a candidate-items list of ten in the continuation session. User triaged the candidate list down to the recommended set (C-1, C-2, C-3, H-2, H-3 — skipping H-1, M-1, M-2, L-1, L-2 per rationale documented in the session summary). All six items accepted at Option A. Q1 extends SPECIFICATION/ to admit nested sub-specifications under SPECIFICATION/templates/<name>/ (closes template-prompt-authoring; opens sub-spec-structural-formalization; does NOT re-open the Multi-specification per project non-goal). Q2 codifies an explicit bootstrap-exception clause naming first seed as the boundary. Q3 adds a one-time 7-step manual initial-vendoring procedure resolving the jsoncomment-self-dependency circularity. Q4 closes both returns-pyright-plugin-disposition (vendor the plugin as sixth vendored lib) and basedpyright-vs-pyright (stay on pyright) at spec level with concrete choices + rationale. Q5 adds a dedicated prompt-QA tier at tests/prompts/<template>/ distinct from the E2E harness; opens prompt-qa-harness deferred entry. Q6 formalizes the companion-doc migration policy with three classes (MIGRATED-to-SPEC-file / SUPERSEDED-by-section / ARCHIVE-ONLY) + per-doc assignment table. 4 careful-review passes + 1 dedicated deferred-items-consistency pass caught 10 inconsistencies cumulatively (6 + 1 + 2 + 0 + 1). Plan file (PLAN_TO_BOOTSTRAP_SPECIFICATION_AND_REPO.md) updated for Q2-Q6 plan-side implications (Q1 plan alignment was already in place from the prior session).

turns.summary: Read the continuation prompt. Verified via diff -q that PROPOSAL.md is byte-identical to history/v017/PROPOSAL.md. Loaded required reading (PROPOSAL.md v017, livespec-nlspec-spec.md, deferred-items.md, python-skill-script-style-requirements.md, goals-and-non-goals.md, subdomains-and-unsolved-routing.md, prior-art.md, 2026-04-19-nlspec-terminology-and-structure-summary.md, history/README.md, the v017 revision file, the PLAN_TO_BOOTSTRAP_SPECIFICATION_AND_REPO.md, and the in-progress critique file with Q1 captured). Created 11 tracking tasks for the full dogfood lifecycle.

Summarized current state to user: Q1 is already captured with Option A (template sub-specifications) as recommended disposition; bootstrap plan is already Q1-Option-A aligned; 10 candidate items from the bootstrap-plan-review were presented with per-item assessment. Recommended promoting C-1 (self-application bootstrap exception), C-2 (initial-vendoring mechanism), C-3 (typechecker + returns-plugin closure), H-2 (seed-prompt LLM round-trip verification), and H-3 (companion-doc migration policy) as Q2-Q6; recommended skipping H-1, M-1, M-2, L-1, L-2 per documented rationale (H-1 is already documented as a commit convention; M-1 is handled by mise; M-2 is intentional deferral to external authority; L-1 is arguably out of scope for a skill-bundle contract; L-2 is handled mechanically by Phase 0 freeze).

turns.content: My recommended set (Recommended): Promote C-1, C-2, C-3, H-2, H-3 as Q2-Q6 (in that order). Skip H-1, M-1, M-2, L-1, L-2 per my rationale above.

turns.summary: Confirmed the promotion set. Appended Q2-Q6 as ## Proposal sections to the in-progress critique file at proposed_changes/v018-proposed-change-proposal-critique-v17.md, updating the opening summary and failure-mode tally (Q1 major gap; Q2-Q4 critical execution-blocking; Q5-Q6 high quality/rework risk). Each proposal section carries target-spec-files, failure-mode-labeled summary, motivation, and proposed changes with Option A (Recommended), B, C, and occasionally a closing rationale for the recommendation.

Q1 interview: restated the built-in templates' under-specification (prompt interview flow, NLSpec-discipline internalization, heading taxonomy, delimiter-comment format, etc. are all implementer-choice today); restated the three options (A nested sub-specifications; B lift Multi-specification non-goal wholesale for livespec repo; C expand main spec.md/contracts.md). Recommended Q1-A — matches livespec self-application, narrow scope expansion, keeps governed-loop invariant. Bootstrap plan is already Q1-Option-A aligned.

turns.content: Accept A (Recommended): Extend SPECIFICATION/ to admit nested sub-specifications under SPECIFICATION/templates/<name>/.

turns.summary: Locked Q1 to A. Moved to Q2: PROPOSAL.md §'Self-application' mandates dogfooding but the first landing of propose-change/revise themselves has nowhere to flow through; the bootstrap plan's steps 2 and 4 imply an imperative bootstrap exception but it's unstated. Recommended Q2-A: add explicit bootstrap-exception clause naming first seed as the boundary; applies ONCE per repo at initial bootstrap; does NOT apply to v2+ releases.

turns.content: Accept A (Recommended): Add explicit bootstrap-exception clause to PROPOSAL.md Self-application naming first seed as the boundary.

turns.summary: Locked Q2 to A. Moved to Q3: `just vendor-update <lib>` depends on `livespec.parse.jsonc` which imports the vendored `jsoncomment`; first-time vendoring of `jsoncomment` has no documented path. Recommended Q3-A: 7-step one-time manual initial-vendoring procedure (git clone + checkout + copy source + copy LICENSE + record in .vendor.jsonc + delete clone + smoke-test import) applied ONCE at Phase 2 of bootstrap plan; post-bootstrap all upstream-sourced-lib mutations flow through `just vendor-update`; shims continue the separate 'widen manually via code review' rule.

turns.content: Accept A (Recommended): Add 7-step one-time manual initial-vendoring procedure to PROPOSAL.md Vendoring discipline (and mirror in style doc).

turns.summary: Locked Q3 to A. Moved to Q4: both `returns-pyright-plugin-disposition` (v007) and `basedpyright-vs-pyright` (v012) are deferred with tradeoffs but no objective closure criteria; the recently-revised bootstrap plan (commit b041d19) pushed both to Phase 1 but PROPOSAL.md didn't reflect this. Recommended Q4-A: decide both at v018 spec level — vendor the dry-python/returns pyright plugin alongside the library at `_vendor/returns_pyright_plugin/` with `[tool.pyright]` pluginPaths; stay on pyright (not basedpyright) because v012 L1+L2 manual strict-plus already covers every diagnostic and community-fork risk outweighs defaults-simplification. Close both deferred items; PROPOSAL.md + style doc + pyproject.toml carry the decisions with rationale. Matches 'Specify architecture, not mechanism' — typechecker-plus-plugin IS architecture (affects public-API type guarantees pervasively).

turns.content: Accept A (Recommended): Vendor the returns pyright plugin; stay on pyright. Close both deferred items in v018.

turns.summary: Locked Q4 to A. Moved to Q5: the `livespec` template's prompts (more authoring complexity than minimal's) have no verification mechanism before the real-tier E2E test runs; under Q1-Option-A, Phase 7's agent-generation step consumes prompt output that has no automatic regression test. Recommended Q5-A: add a dedicated prompt-QA tier at tests/prompts/<template>/ with a small deterministic harness scope-distinct from tests/e2e/fake_claude.py; replays prompt-response pairs; asserts on structured output at the input-schema boundary PLUS declared semantic properties. Every built-in template ships ≥1 prompt-QA test per REQUIRED prompt. Runs per-commit in `just check`. Opens new `prompt-qa-harness` deferred entry joint-resolved with `sub-spec-structural-formalization` (supersedes closed `template-prompt-authoring` for prompt authoring) and `end-to-end-integration-test`.

turns.content: Accept A (Recommended): Add dedicated prompt-QA tier at tests/prompts/ with its own small deterministic harness.

turns.summary: Locked Q5 to A. Moved to Q6: `companion-docs-mapping` deferred entry uses 'or similar' hedging for destinations; two implementers produce non-identical seeded specs; Phase 8 auditability requires per-doc precision before the cycle starts. Recommended Q6-A: formalize migration-class policy (MIGRATED-to-SPEC-file / SUPERSEDED-by-section / ARCHIVE-ONLY) + per-doc assignment table in PROPOSAL.md §'Self-application'. Table covers every companion doc with class + destination + target phase annotation.

turns.content: Accept A (Recommended): Formalize migration-class policy (MIGRATED / SUPERSEDED / ARCHIVE-ONLY) + table assigning every companion doc to class + destination in PROPOSAL.md.

turns.summary: Locked Q6 to A. Interview complete — all six items accepted at Option A. Proceeded to the dogfood lifecycle apply.
